tweets.text:
The Morning Minute (7.22)

⏰Top News:
-Crypto majors mostly flat, HYPE falls 6%; BTC even at $66k
-BTC ETFs see another $203M in net inflows, $930M over past 6 sessions
-Jack Mallers steps down from Twenty One Capital with stock down 90%
-Telegram announces new non-custodial wallet, calls it largest rollout in history
-Senate Democrats balk at latest Clarity Act ethics deal progress

🌎 Macro Crypto and Markets
-Crypto majors are mostly flat, Hype sells off; BTC -0.2% at $66k; ETH even at $1,930; SOL even at $78; HYPE -6% at $58.80
-JTO (+5%), GRAM (+4%) and PYTH (+3%) led top movers
-Oil +3.5% at $87; Gold +1% at $4,120
-Stock futures are red as oil continues to rise; DOW -0.1%, Nasdaq -0.7%

-Senate Democrats balked at the Trump ethics deal, with the sticking point now who enforces the ban; Democrats want state attorneys general, while the White House insists on the US attorney general

-The White House pushed Senate Democrats to accept what it called “the most comprehensive and wide-ranging ethics provision in history,” while arguing Democrats would be to blame if CLARITY stalls

-Kraken is expanding xStocks into Hong Kong, the UK and South Korea, partnering with GTN for investment infrastructure

-The S&P Dow Jones and Pantera launched a new Digital Asset Index focused on projects with revenue generation and fundamentals; top holdings include ETH, BNB, SOL, TRX and HYPE

-Movement Labs filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, months after the token scandal that upended the project and after raising over $40M in private funding

Corporate Treasuries & ETFs
-The Bitcoin ETFs saw $203M in net inflows on Tuesday, now with 6 straight green sessions; the ETH ETFs saw $38M in inflows
-Jack Mallers stepped down as Twenty One Capital CEO as Tether’s three-way Bitcoin merger collapsed; XXI dropped 18% to a record low, now off more than 80% from its highs despite holding 43,514 BTC

Meme Coin Tracker
-Meme leaders were slightly red; DOGE -1%, SHIB -1%, PEPE -4%, PENGU -2%, TRUMP +1%, BONK -1%
-Robinhood chain was led by WOOD (+40%), r0b (+800x), and Wallet (+20%); Cashcat -18% to $65M and PONS +5% to $30M lead
-Solana leaders included Jimothy (+55%), HeavyPulp (+120%) and KET (+220%); ANSEM -12% to $195M

💰 Token, Airdrop & Protocol Tracker
-Telegram's Pavel Durov announced a native non-custodial Gram wallet for every Telegram app this summer, calling it the largest non-custodial wallet rollout in history, with instant zero-fee transactions for over a billion users
-Augustus raised $180 million to build a clearing bank for the AI and stablecoin era
-Fomo App broke into the top 20 in the Apple app store Finance rankings
-Pump Fun introduced a new BOOST feature for its launch mechanic that will provide more liquidity for every bonded coin

🚚 What is happening in NFTs?
-NFT leaders were mixed; Punks even at 32 ETH, BAYC -1% at 8.65 ETH, Pudgy +1% at 4.18 ETH; Hypurr’s -3% at 185 HYPE
-Projet Aeon (+30%) and Max Pain (+17%) led top movers
-Robinhood NFTs saw big movement with pyopyo +266% to 0.07 ETH; Cash Cats +265%, OnChainHoodies +70% and Gremlin Cartel +60%

BREAKING: Bitcoin ETFs attracted +$900 million in inflows last week, the largest weekly inflow since early May.

This marks a sharp acceleration from +$197 million in inflows in the prior week.

The largest Bitcoin ETF, $IBIT, led the surge, attracting +$193 million last week, after bringing in +$282 million the week before.

Subsequently, on Monday and Tuesday, $IBIT saw additional inflows of +$115 million and +$164 million.

This brings the total $IBIT inflows to +$501 million so far in July, on track for the largest monthly intake since April and the 3rd-largest this year.

Investor demand for Bitcoin funds is rapidly improving.

tweets.text:
It's official:

The US 30Y Note Yield has now traded above 5.00% for the longest stretch since 2007.

So far this year, the 30Y Note Yield has traded above 5.00% for 27 days, or ~19% of all trading sessions.

Just months ago, markets saw 3+ rate cuts in 2026.

Talk about a turn of events.

tweets.text: On this day in 1944, delegates from 44 nations signed the Bretton Woods agreement at a New Hampshire resort hotel, ending a three-week conference that redesigned the global financial system for the postwar era. The deal pegged the world's major currencies to the U.S. dollar, which was in turn convertible to gold at $35 an ounce, effectively making the dollar the world's reserve currency and cementing American financial dominance. It also created two institutions that still shape the global economy: the International Monetary Fund, to stabilize exchange rates and lend to countries in crisis, and the World Bank, to finance postwar reconstruction and development. The gold peg collapsed in 1971 when Nixon closed the gold window, but the dollar's central role never went away. Roughly 58% of the world's foreign exchange reserves are still held in dollars today.
tweets.full_text: On this day in 1944, delegates from 44 nations signed the Bretton Woods agreement at a New Hampshire resort hotel, ending a three-week conference that redesigned the global financial system for the postwar era. The deal pegged the world's major currencies to the U.S. dollar, which was in turn convertible to gold at $35 an ounce, effectively making the dollar the world's reserve currency and cementing American financial dominance. It also created two institutions that still shape the global economy: the International Monetary Fund, to stabilize exchange rates and lend to countries in crisis, and the World Bank, to finance postwar reconstruction and development. The gold peg collapsed in 1971 when Nixon closed the gold window, but the dollar's central role never went away. Roughly 58% of the world's foreign exchange reserves are still held in dollars today.

tweets.text:
🇨🇳 Chinese Banks, Insurers Increase A-Share Investment - China Securities Journal

➡ Chinese banks and insurers are stepping up equity investment and dividend payout, reflecting confidence in the long-term outlook for the economy and capital markets, China Securities Journal says in a report.

➡ China Life Asset Management bought more than 10 billion yuan ($1.5 billion) worth of equities and equity funds in a single day recently.

➡ Industrial Bank’s subsidiaries in wealth management, trust and funds have significantly expanded products involving equities.

➡ Banks and insurers are also paying out higher dividends to strengthen long-term investor confidence
In a separate report, the paper says that stock ETFs have attracted more than 385 billion yuan in net inflows so far this month.

*Link (Chinese): https://t.co/ov01fsp5M6

tweets.text:
🇨🇳 #China Seen Stepping Up Fiscal Spending in 2H 2026 - Securities Times

*China is expected to accelerate fiscal spending in the second half as substantial budgeted funds and government bond quotas remain unused, Securities Times says in a report.

*Government bond sales were slow in 1H, leaving 2.3 trillion yuan ($340 billion) worth of local government special bonds to be issued in 2H as well as 728 billion yuan worth of special sovereign bonds.

*Economists expect Beijing’s planned 800 billion yuan new policy financing tool to launch in the third quarter to boost major construction projects.

*The government still has additional funds to deploy without raising the budget deficit. It can use money left over from previous years or sell idling land.

*Fiscal policy is likely to take a bigger role in supporting growth in the second half, as monetary policy is constrained by things including the US-China yield gap and banks’ net interest rate margin.

*Link (Chinese): https://t.co/ZwoW1cXMD8

*Economists expect Beijing’s planned 800 billion yuan new policy financing tool to launch in the third quarter to boost major construction projects.

Instead of one actor reading a whole chapter, they have like active conversations in dialog and it really dials up the realism imo.

tweets.text:
⚽🥅The quadrennial curse holds. Every World Cup year — 2014, 2018, 2022, and 2026 YTD — Bitcoin is the single worst-performing asset on the board (−57%, −74%, −64%, −25%). In the off-years it's almost always #1; the only crack came last year. 

Four World Cups, four bottom finishes — though with 2026 only half run, there's still time to break the streak.
tweets.full_text:

tweets.text:
Hong Kong stocks finally showed a pulse.

More than 55% of Hang Seng stocks are above their 50 day averages after fewer than 10% were there in late June.

When this happened with the index down 18% or more from a one year high, the win rate was 80%+.
The misses were mostly systemic shocks like the Global Financial Crisis.

Read full analysis: https://t.co/uhRQavYvXh

tweets.text:
July 21, 2026: McClellan Summation Index closed at 262 with SPX near highs at 7509.2.

Breadth is confirming the uptrend, but not decisively.

Near these highs, that distinction matters. If MSI keeps rising, the breakout gains broader support. If it stalls, the rally remains more dependent on narrow leadership.

Track whether the SPX rally is broadening or narrowing with McClellan Summation Index: https://t.co/xxRX5UyILt
